title: audit — Eloquent observers
description: Two observers — one per owned entity. AuditObserver is the heavyweight — it enforces the four immutability invariants (require tenant_id, refuse update, refuse delete outside purge, populate application_id from request context). AuditRetentionPolicyObserver enforces the Enterprise-only entitlement gate + retention_years cap + date-range shape.

handles: creating — refuse when tenant_id IS NULL (defense-in-depth against vendor drift; the vendor observer does not know about our tenant_id column). Throws NOTIFICATIONS_AUDIT_TENANT_ID_MISSING with 500 status (this is an internal invariant violation, not a user error).

creating — when application_id IS NULL AND request context has an application (via X-Application-Id middleware), populate application_id from the resolved application. When request context has no application (platform-plane audit) leave application_id NULL.

creating — validate `event` against the closed set: vendor-defined ('created', 'updated', 'deleted', 'restored') + our registered custom events (loaded from data/audited-events-catalog.json at boot). Refuse unknown events with NOTIFICATIONS_AUDIT_UNKNOWN_EVENT_TYPE.

creating — if config.audit.compliance.redact_pii_in_old_new_values is true (tenant opt-in), run the registered PiiRedactor over old_values + new_values before persistence. Default is false — regulators need full context.

created — fire AuditRecorded event afterCommit. Payload is intentionally narrow (audit_id + tenant_id + application_id + event + auditable_type + auditable_id + user info + created_at) — NO diff blobs to prevent leaking PII into event queues.
created — write monitoring metric increment: `academorix.audit.records_total{tenant_id, event, auditable_type}`. Counter, not gauge — cumulative record count.

updating — REFUSE ALL when config.audit.immutability.forbid_update is true (default). Throws NOTIFICATIONS_AUDIT_ROW_IMMUTABLE (422). Audit rows are immutable after write. Test-env override: config.audit.test.forbid_update_relaxed can relax this for unit tests that fabricate specific states.

deleting — REFUSE unless the caller is the PurgeExpiredAuditsJob service account. The check is: (a) config.audit.immutability.forbid_delete_outside_purge is true (default) AND (b) request context is a queued job AND (c) the job class matches PurgeExpiredAuditsJob::class. Throws NOTIFICATIONS_AUDIT_ROW_DELETE_FORBIDDEN (403) otherwise.
creating — refuse when tenant lacks audit_retention_extended entitlement (Enterprise-only). Throws NOTIFICATIONS_AUDIT_RETENTION_NOT_ENTITLED (403).

creating — refuse when tenant already has an active policy (deleted_at IS NULL). Throws NOTIFICATIONS_AUDIT_RETENTION_POLICY_ALREADY_ACTIVE (409). Caller must PATCH the existing policy instead of POSTing a second one.
creating — validate retention_years is in [1..config.audit.retention.max_years]. Throws NOTIFICATIONS_AUDIT_INVALID_RETENTION_YEARS (422).
creating — validate override_reason is a non-empty string (10-500 chars). Throws NOTIFICATIONS_AUDIT_INVALID_OVERRIDE_REASON (422).
creating — validate override_starts_at is <= override_ends_at (when override_ends_at is not null). Throws NOTIFICATIONS_AUDIT_INVALID_OVERRIDE_DATES (422).
creating — set override_starts_at = now() when not caller-supplied.
created — fire AuditRetentionPolicyCreated event afterCommit.
created — dispatch AuditRetentionPolicyExtendedNotification to tenant compliance officer + owner.
created — invalidate the per-tenant retention cache (audit:retention:{tenant_id}) so PurgeExpiredAuditsJob picks up the new cutoff on next run.
updating — validate all the same rules as creating (retention_years range, override_reason shape, date consistency). Refuses via NOTIFICATIONS_AUDIT_INVALID_* on violation.
updating — refuse changes to tenant_id column (immutable — the policy belongs to the tenant that created it; caller must delete + recreate for a different tenant).
updated — fire AuditRetentionPolicyUpdated event with the list of changed columns.
updated — invalidate the retention cache.
deleting — soft-delete only. Resets retention_years to config.audit.retention.default_years (7) so future purges honor the fallback.
